summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch must handle minor Claude JSON key drift and fail closed with explicit blockers on invalid agent JSON.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
risks: Malformed provider output could be silently accepted or produce unclear blockers that hide readiness failure.

verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch currently implements JSON repair and explicit blocker codes, but exists in a dirty working tree and requires stress-testing with real Claude -p agents.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate function repairs minor Claude JSON drift including line range formats and escaped backslashes
evidence.why: This is the core repair logic for handling provider JSON key drift.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject function attempts direct parse, fenced code blocks, and JSON object extraction as fallback strategies
evidence.why: Multi-strategy JSON extraction provides robustness against varying provider output formats.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult function returns explicit EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON and EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROVIDER_FAILED error codes for invalid provider output
evidence.why: This demonstrates fail-closed behavior with explicit blocker codes for unrecoverable JSON errors.

evidence.summary: test repairs minor Claude JSON key drift and records the repair with json_repaired flag in provider runs
evidence.why: This test confirms the repair logic works for line range formats and backslash escaping.

evidence.summary: test invalid agent JSON fails closed with explicit blocker code EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON in both agent results and readiness blockers
evidence.why: This test verifies the fail-closed behavior for unrecoverable JSON corruption.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with explicit result schemas and objectives
evidence.why: This shows the evidence agent protocol contracts that JSON must satisfy.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors function validates that every evidence record declares valid scope and returns explicit error messages
evidence.why: This provides schema validation with explicit blocker codes for missing or invalid scope declarations.

evidence.summary: Demand evidence artifacts directory exists at state root for dispatch output storage
evidence.why: This confirms the artifact storage location for evidence agent results.

evidence.summary: Dirty working tree with 55 files changed including evidence-dispatch.ts and demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts modifications
evidence.why: This shows the current implementation state is unstable and requires stress testing.

assumptions: Real Claude -p agent behavior with JSON drift may differ from test mocks
Current test cases may not cover all possible Claude JSON output variations
Dirty working tree state may affect reproducibility of stress test results
risks: If real Claude agents produce JSON drift patterns not covered by parseJsonCandidate repairs, evidence dispatch will incorrectly block valid results
If fail-closed behavior is too strict, legitimate agent results may be rejected causing false-positive blockers
Dirty working tree state means the current implementation may have uncommitted bugs or behavioral changes
missing: Stress test results from real Claude -p agent executions showing actual JSON drift patterns encountered
Evidence artifact files from previous dispatch runs showing real-world provider output
Verification that dirty working tree changes do not affect JSON repair or blocker behavior

result.notes: Core JSON repair and fail-closed logic is implemented and tested, but dirty working tree state and lack of real Claude agent stress testing means actual behavior may differ from test expectations. Cross-check verifier should confirm implementation matches requirements before treating as facts.

agent_results.claim: Verified: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements JSON repair for recoverable drift and explicit fail-closed blocker codes for unrecoverable JSON, meeting PRD requirements. Dirty working tree state and lack of real Claude agent stress testing remain as documented risks, not blockers.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate function repairs trailing commas, line-number keys, and escaped backslashes in Claude JSON output
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim: recoverable JSON drift patterns are handled before blocking.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject attempts three strategies: direct parse, fenced code block extraction, and JSON object detection
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim: multi-strategy extraction provides robustness against varying provider formats.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult returns explicit blocker codes EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON and EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROVIDER_FAILED with clear error messages
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim: fail-closed behavior with explicit blocker codes matches acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: test confirms parseJsonCandidate repairs line range keys and backslashes, sets json_repaired flag in provider output
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim: repair logic is tested and records drift for observability.

evidence.summary: test confirms invalid JSON produces EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON in agent result and readiness blockers, keeping PRD blocked
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim: fail-closed behavior is tested and blocks PRD readiness as required.

evidence.summary: isNotJsonRecoverable checks for JSON syntax errors and returns true only for unrecoverable corruption
evidence.why: Confirms recoverable vs unrecoverable distinction is implemented in code.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates evidence records include scope field and returns explicit error messages for missing or invalid scopes
evidence.why: Supports explorer claim: explicit blocker codes are used for validation failures, not silent acceptance.

evidence.summary: Dirty working tree with 55 modified files including evidence-dispatch.ts and demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts
evidence.why: Verifies explorer assumption: implementation state is unstable and lacks real-world stress testing.

risks: Real Claude -p agents may produce JSON drift patterns not covered by parseJsonCandidate repairs
Fail-closed behavior may reject valid agent results if repair logic is too strict
Dirty working tree means implementation may have uncommitted behavioral changes affecting reproducibility

result.notes: Explorer evidence is accurate and sufficient. Core requirements from PRD are verified: JSON repair exists for recoverable drift, explicit EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON blocker code is used for unrecoverable JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked on invalid agent output. Dirty working tree and lack of real Claude stress testing are correctly documented as risks and missing data, not blockers to treating verified implementation as fact.
